title: Ebi Showcase quality gate catalog
description: Single source of quality expectations for humans, Ralph, deterministic checkers, and LLM review lenses. AGENTS.md points here; detailed pedagogy stays in linked docs.

meters.playable: Demo launches and can be played (208/208 gated + VFX side count).
meters.advanced_quality: Finished genre game has replay, controls, feedback, mobile, bilingual polish.
meters.authoring: Learner can find a file, add one RULE, and verify it.

loop_principles.update: Update owns input, rules, and state transitions at the engine's configured update cadence; never assume a universal 60Hz value.
loop_principles.draw: Draw is a side-effect-free projection of the latest state. Its frequency, timing, order relative to Update, and visual style are independent implementation details.
loop_principles.multiple_renderers: One state may be projected in any number of visual forms. Changing Draw does not change Update, input, physics, or rules; learner-facing pages demonstrate that freedom visually instead of prescribing a list of styles.
language_to_avoid: Update is always immediately followed by Draw
the game runs at exactly 60 FPS

gates.summary: Update owns all state mutation and all input reading.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the page claim or show input/score/collision updates outside Update (or a pure helper Update calls)? Flag Draw or passive prose that puts rules in Draw.

gates.summary: Draw only projects the current game struct to pixels.
gates.prompt_hint: Does Draw decide outcomes, read input, roll RNG, or mutate game fields? Quote the offending line or paragraph.

gates.summary: Draw is a referentially transparent projection of game, assets, and viewport.
gates.prompt_hint: Does Draw write hidden state, mutate caches or slices, advance time, consume randomness, read input, trigger FX/gameplay transitions, or otherwise change the environment? Local derived values and temporary DrawImageOptions are allowed; persistent changes belong in Update.

gates.summary: Each renderer is a deterministic projection of game state; the state itself is not stored in the screen.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the lesson imply Draw invents state or that two draws with the same state and the same renderer may differ without an explicit visual effect? Also allow different renderers to show the same state differently.

gates.summary: Gameplay advances in Update at the engine's configured tick; it does not depend on Draw frequency.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the lesson distinguish the engine's Update cadence from rendering cadence and state that gameplay must not depend on Draw? Ebitengine commonly uses a 60-tick-per-second default, but do not present 60Hz as a universal promise or require a fixed number. Flag wording that teaches Update and Draw as alternating equal phases, immediately paired calls, or puts progression in Draw.

gates.summary: Update does not depend on Draw's timing, order, or visual representation.
gates.prompt_hint: Flag wording that says Update must be immediately followed by Draw, or that gameplay requires a particular renderer. Also flag a learner-facing explanation that teaches renderer freedom only by listing style names: show the same live state or a concrete visual comparison instead. The displayed views are examples, never a required set or a limit.

gates.summary: Early loop pages surface the Update/Draw axiom (contract block or clear 3-way wording).
gates.prompt_hint: Core LEVEL 01–03 and Build Track should restate Update owns mutation, Draw projects, Draw does not write.

gates.summary: Challenge names a repository-relative Go path (or local file to create).
gates.prompt_hint: Is there an exact path like games/.../main.go or graduation/... for the learner to open?

gates.summary: Primary challenge is YOUR FIRST RULE (or equivalent), not tuning alone.
gates.prompt_hint: Is the main challenge a branch/counter/state/data change rather than only changing a constant?

gates.summary: Challenge names how to verify (go test, go run, or visible browser check).
gates.prompt_hint: Does the RULE text say go test, go run, or an observable before/after check?

gates.summary: RULE lives in Update or a pure helper Update calls—never as Draw mutation.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the challenge tell the learner to change Draw to implement the rule?

gates.summary: Thin-wrapper tracks show editable entry + path-labelled internal excerpt.
gates.prompt_hint: For shared-engine tracks, is the short main.go shown before a labelled internal excerpt?

gates.summary: Build Track and graduation do not require cloning this repo as a prerequisite.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the page say the learner must clone EbiShowcase before starting?

gates.summary: OGP/Twitter descriptions do not use the legacy「値を変えて」authoring promise.
gates.prompt_hint: OGP must promise adding a Go rule, not only changing a value.

gates.summary: One main idea (plus at most one supporting idea) per step.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the page try to teach several unrelated systems at once without ordering them?

gates.summary: Lead with something the learner can see or do before naming abstractions.
gates.prompt_hint: Inspect the rendered reading order, not only headings. Does the introduction lead directly to the playable demo before glossary cards, code, diagrams, or an unrelated setup branch? Does every named character appear visually in the same introductory context, or does the copy clearly say that early shape-only levels precede the character?

gates.summary: DEEP DIVE explains the named mechanic, not only controls.
gates.prompt_hint: Is DEEP DIVE mostly a control list without data/state explanation?

gates.summary: REAL GO / code-lesson matches the code learners can open.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the snippet invent a standalone loop while main.go only calls a shared Run(Config)?

gates.summary: Japanese avoids unexplained jargon; uses concrete primary-school-friendly examples.
gates.prompt_hint: Are technical terms dumped without a pictureable example?

gates.summary: Genre steps do not re-teach Update/Draw as if LEVEL 01 did not exist.
gates.prompt_hint: Does a mid-track page reintroduce the entire game loop from zero?

gates.summary: Lesson page includes DEEP DIVE.
Lesson page includes concept-row.
Lesson page includes motion-lab / data-lab.
Lesson page includes code-lesson.
Lesson page includes why-grid.
Lesson page embeds the play/<slug>/ iframe (or shared game.html).
Lesson page loads learn.js.
Core LEVEL pages embed full main.go with map and copy button.
Expected main.go exists for the curriculum entry.
Gated playable count remains 208; VFX counted separately.
Every curriculum route has JA and EN pages.
Home cards map to real WebP gameplay captures.
In-page links stay relative for GitHub Pages project hosting.
gates.prompt_hint: Are there absolute site-root paths that would break under /REPO/?

gates.summary: Content pages carry page-specific OGP + Twitter card tags.
Never write “Ebi Boy”; use 海老・天次郎 / Ebi Tenjiroh.
Famous titles are genre signposts only; no copied names, art, or scenarios.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the page present itself as a clone or reuse protected names as the product title?

gates.summary: Playable iframes have a meaningful title attribute.
Status is not conveyed by color alone.
gates.prompt_hint: Is success/failure only described as a color change with no text or shape?

gates.summary: Keyboard/pointer and touch complete the game loop.
gates.prompt_hint: Browser audit: both desktop and phone-size controls finish a run.

gates.summary: Final games have score/time/grade/unlock or similar replay hook.
Advanced FX remakes keep play entities separate from fx.System.
gates.prompt_hint: Does the lesson mix win/lose rules into the FX-only layer?

gates.summary: Basic VFX steps stay drawing toys; no artificial win/lose only to look like genre lessons.
